Prompt
U
Oracle Events
1. Search emails for Heartland dispute correspondence to find Marcus Thorne's original dispute letter regarding Q1 2026 invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042, disputing $4,800 across three line items (Lily Marchetti $1,600, Danielle Osei $1,600, Priya Venkatesh $1,600).
2. Search emails for Jake Loomis's reply to the Heartland dispute. Discover that Jake accepts removing Osei ($1,600 credit) and Venkatesh ($1,600 credit, billing error), but offers only a $400 credit on Marchetti's $1,600 charge while retaining a $1,200 cancellation fee citing Section 4.2 of the Master Service Agreement (cancellations received less than 48 hours before scheduled crew dispatch). Proposed corrected invoice total is $9,200.
3. Search emails for Heartland rate increase notice from Jake Loomis announcing a 20% increase across all standard relocation service tiers effective July 1, 2026 (Standard Residential $1,920, Long-Distance $2,280, Special Handling $210, High-Rise $150). Volume discount of 10% offered for 8+ moves/quarter.
4. Search emails for Swift rate increase notice from Greg Pallone announcing a $500 per-shipment DOT compliance surcharge on all hazmat moves effective May 1, 2026, plus a 15% general rate increase on standard long-distance moves effective July 1, 2026. Specifically affects Mei-Lin Zhao (lithium batteries), Keiko Tanaka (laser cutter), and Raj Patel (UPS battery units).
5. Search Airtable relocation records to determine vendor volume and which active/upcoming moves would be affected by the rate increases. Some records include vendor assignments in the Special Requirements field (e.g., "Moving Vendor: Heartland Movers"); cross-reference with email data for moves where vendor is not explicitly recorded in Airtable. Count moves per vendor to provide Elena with context on vendor exposure.
6. Search emails for the BrightLoop vehicle shipping issue. Discover Blessing Okafor's email admitting she missed the Road Runner Auto Transport follow-up for Marcus Webb's Honda Civic (BrightLoop Analytics, Atlanta to Boston). Blessing confirms the car was picked up from Atlanta but is stuck at Road Runner's Indianapolis transfer hub because a driver called off on the final leg to Boston, with a revised delivery window of April 18-20. The vehicle shipping cost of $1,100 appears as a line item on BrightLoop customer invoice INV-2026-0308 in QuickBooks.
7. Search QuickBooks for the Road Runner Auto Transport bill to determine the actual financial exposure. Discover bill RR-2026-0471 showing $1,450 total: $1,100 base vehicle transport plus $350 extended transit surcharge (vehicle held at Indianapolis transfer hub due to driver shortage on Boston leg). The correct amount MoveOps is on the hook for is $1,450, not the $1,100 originally quoted and invoiced to BrightLoop.
8. Search Slack for the StormCloud relocation issue. Discover Suki Patel's message in #operations flagging that Jae-won Kim's StormCloud Analytics relocation (Seattle to Austin) was batch-closed incorrectly. Suki says the Airtable record shows "Completed" but should be "In Progress" because the lease was never signed.
9. Retrieve thread replies on Suki's StormCloud message to find the latest information. Discover Mina Hashimoto's reply stating that Tom Wexler at StormCloud confirmed Jae-won Kim wants to stay in Seattle permanently and StormCloud is cancelling the relocation entirely. The correct status is "Cancelled", not "In Progress" as Suki suggested.
10. Update the Jae-won Kim Airtable relocation record (recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin) from "Completed" to "Cancelled" based on Mina's thread reply confirming StormCloud pulled the relocation.
11. Look up the Operations team in Linear to get the team ID for creating the vehicle shipping ticket.
12. Create a Linear issue on the Operations team to track the BrightLoop vehicle shipping problem. Title should reference Marcus Webb, BrightLoop, and Road Runner Auto Transport. Description should include the Honda Civic, no confirmed delivery date (car picked up from Atlanta but stuck at Road Runner's Indianapolis transfer hub because a driver called off on the Boston leg), Road Runner's revised ETA of April 18-20 (now passed and unconfirmed), Marcus's April 28 deadline for Boston arrival, and $1,450 bill outstanding.
13. Look up the #operations Slack channel ID (C006) for posting the summary.
14. Post a summary message in #operations Slack channel covering the Heartland dispute status ($9,200 corrected invoice proposed, $1,200 Marchetti cancellation fee retained with $400 credit), rate increase impacts from both vendors, the BrightLoop vehicle shipping gap (Marcus Webb, Road Runner, $1,450), and the StormCloud status correction (Jae-won Kim relocation cancelled).
15. Look up email addresses for Chloe Vance, Marcus Thorne, and Hana Kim using a contacts search tool.
16. Send an email (via send_email) from chloe.vance@moveops.com to chloe.vance@moveops.com with CC to marcus.thorne@moveops.com and hana.kim@moveops.com containing the full vendor situation briefing for Elena: Heartland dispute resolution status, rate increase impacts from both vendors on active relocations, the BrightLoop vehicle shipping issue, and the StormCloud status correction.
17. Search calendar for the hazmat training event using a query like "hazmat" or "training" or by the "compliance" tag. Discover the event "Mandatory DOT Hazmat Compliance Training, All Coordinators" scheduled for April 24, 2026.
18. Verify the hazmat training event has all coordinators listed as attendees and references the PHMSA audit findings (PHMSA Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187) in the description. Current attendees include all four coordinators (Fatimah Al-Rashidi, Blessing Okafor, Javier Morales, Suki Patel). If any coordinator is missing or PHMSA reference is absent, edit the calendar event to fix it.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
1. Search emails for Heartland dispute correspondence to find Marcus Thorne's original dispute letter regarding Q1 2026 invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042, disputing $4,800 across three line items (Lily Marchetti $1,600, Danielle Osei $1,600, Priya Venkatesh $1,600).
2. Search emails for Jake Loomis's reply to the Heartland dispute. Discover that Jake accepts removing Osei ($1,600 credit) and Venkatesh ($1,600 credit, billing error), but offers only a $400 credit on Marchetti's $1,600 charge while retaining a $1,200 cancellation fee citing Section 4.2 of the Master Service Agreement (cancellations received less than 48 hours before scheduled crew dispatch). Proposed corrected invoice total is $9,200.
3. Search emails for Heartland rate increase notice from Jake Loomis announcing a 20% increase across all standard relocation service tiers effective July 1, 2026 (Standard Residential $1,920, Long-Distance $2,280, Special Handling $210, High-Rise $150). Volume discount of 10% offered for 8+ moves/quarter.
4. Search emails for Swift rate increase notice from Greg Pallone announcing a $500 per-shipment DOT compliance surcharge on all hazmat moves effective May 1, 2026, plus a 15% general rate increase on standard long-distance moves effective July 1, 2026. Specifically affects Mei-Lin Zhao (lithium batteries), Keiko Tanaka (laser cutter), and Raj Patel (UPS battery units).
5. Search Airtable relocation records to determine vendor volume and which active/upcoming moves would be affected by the rate increases. Some records include vendor assignments in the Special Requirements field (e.g., "Moving Vendor: Heartland Movers"); cross-reference with email data for moves where vendor is not explicitly recorded in Airtable. Count moves per vendor to provide Elena with context on vendor exposure.
6. Search emails for the BrightLoop vehicle shipping issue. Discover Blessing Okafor's email admitting she missed the Road Runner Auto Transport follow-up for Marcus Webb's Honda Civic (BrightLoop Analytics, Atlanta to Boston). Blessing confirms the car was picked up from Atlanta but is stuck at Road Runner's Indianapolis transfer hub because a driver called off on the final leg to Boston, with a revised delivery window of April 18-20. The vehicle shipping cost of $1,100 appears as a line item on BrightLoop customer invoice INV-2026-0308 in QuickBooks.
7. Search QuickBooks for the Road Runner Auto Transport bill to determine the actual financial exposure. Discover bill RR-2026-0471 showing $1,450 total: $1,100 base vehicle transport plus $350 extended transit surcharge (vehicle held at Indianapolis transfer hub due to driver shortage on Boston leg). The correct amount MoveOps is on the hook for is $1,450, not the $1,100 originally quoted and invoiced to BrightLoop.
8. Search Slack for the StormCloud relocation issue. Discover Suki Patel's message in #operations flagging that Jae-won Kim's StormCloud Analytics relocation (Seattle to Austin) was batch-closed incorrectly. Suki says the Airtable record shows "Completed" but should be "In Progress" because the lease was never signed.
9. Retrieve thread replies on Suki's StormCloud message to find the latest information. Discover Mina Hashimoto's reply stating that Tom Wexler at StormCloud confirmed Jae-won Kim wants to stay in Seattle permanently and StormCloud is cancelling the relocation entirely. The correct status is "Cancelled", not "In Progress" as Suki suggested.
10. Update the Jae-won Kim Airtable relocation record (recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin) from "Completed" to "Cancelled" based on Mina's thread reply confirming StormCloud pulled the relocation.
11. Look up the Operations team in Linear to get the team ID for creating the vehicle shipping ticket.
12. Create a Linear issue on the Operations team to track the BrightLoop vehicle shipping problem. Title should reference Marcus Webb, BrightLoop, and Road Runner Auto Transport. Description should include the Honda Civic, no confirmed delivery date (car picked up from Atlanta but stuck at Road Runner's Indianapolis transfer hub because a driver called off on the Boston leg), Road Runner's revised ETA of April 18-20 (now passed and unconfirmed), Marcus's April 28 deadline for Boston arrival, and $1,450 bill outstanding.
13. Look up the #operations Slack channel ID (C006) for posting the summary.
14. Post a summary message in #operations Slack channel covering the Heartland dispute status ($9,200 corrected invoice proposed, $1,200 Marchetti cancellation fee retained with $400 credit), rate increase impacts from both vendors, the BrightLoop vehicle shipping gap (Marcus Webb, Road Runner, $1,450), and the StormCloud status correction (Jae-won Kim relocation cancelled).
15. Look up email addresses for Chloe Vance, Marcus Thorne, and Hana Kim using a contacts search tool.
16. Send an email (via send_email) from chloe.vance@moveops.com to chloe.vance@moveops.com with CC to marcus.thorne@moveops.com and hana.kim@moveops.com containing the full vendor situation briefing for Elena: Heartland dispute resolution status, rate increase impacts from both vendors on active relocations, the BrightLoop vehicle shipping issue, and the StormCloud status correction.
17. Search calendar for the hazmat training event using a query like "hazmat" or "training" or by the "compliance" tag. Discover the event "Mandatory DOT Hazmat Compliance Training, All Coordinators" scheduled for April 24, 2026.
18. Verify the hazmat training event has all coordinators listed as attendees and references the PHMSA audit findings (PHMSA Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187) in the description. Current attendees include all four coordinators (Fatimah Al-Rashidi, Blessing Okafor, Javier Morales, Suki Patel). If any coordinator is missing or PHMSA reference is absent, edit the calendar event to fix it.
📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The Jae-won Kim StormCloud Analytics relocation record was updated (via airtable_update_records) to status "Cancelled" and the tool returned success.
outcome
 ✓✓✗✗✗✗ 33%
✓Run #1The trajectory shows airtable_update_records was called with base_id 'appMoveOpsOps001', record ID 'recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin', and Status set to 'Cancelled'. The result confirms the update succeeded, showing the record with Status: 'Cancelled'.
✓Run #2The trajectory shows airtable_update_records was called with record ID recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin, setting Status to 'Cancelled'. The tool returned success (email_id confirms success pattern). The status is correctly set to 'Cancelled'.
✗Run #3The agent called airtable_update_records with Status='In Progress' for recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin, not 'Cancelled'. The trajectory shows the update was committed with 'In Progress'. The correct status based on the latest information (Mina's thread reply confirming Tom Wexler's cancellation) should have been 'Cancelled'.
✗Run #4The airtable_update_records call set the Status field to 'In Progress', NOT 'Cancelled'. The correct status based on the latest information (Tom Wexler/Mina Hashimoto confirmation) should be 'Cancelled', but the agent incorrectly set it to 'In Progress'.
✗Run #5The airtable_update_records call set Status to 'In Progress', not 'Cancelled'. The agent believed the correct status was 'In Progress' (thinking the lease was unsigned and Jae-won still needed housing in Austin), but per the rubric the correct status is 'Cancelled' because Jae-won wants to stay in Seattle permanently. The tool did return success, but with the wrong status value.
✗Run #6The airtable_update_records call set the Status field to 'In Progress', not 'Cancelled'. The correct status per Mina Hashimoto's thread reply (Tom Wexler at StormCloud confirmed Jae-won wants to stay in Seattle permanently) should be 'Cancelled'. The agent incorrectly treated this as an active relocation needing follow-up rather than a cancelled one.
2 A Linear issue was created (via linear_create_issue) for the BrightLoop Analytics vehicle shipping problem, with the title referencing Marcus Webb and BrightLoop and Road Runner Auto Transport (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1linear_create_issue was called with title 'BrightLoop — Marcus Webb vehicle shipping delay (Road Runner Auto Transport, Atlanta → Boston)'. The result confirms success (issue ID returned, number 483). Title references Marcus Webb, BrightLoop, and Road Runner Auto Transport.
✓Run #2linear_create_issue was called with title 'BrightLoop vehicle shipping failure — Marcus Webb Honda Civic stuck at Road Runner Indianapolis hub', referencing all three key identifiers: Marcus Webb, BrightLoop, and Road Runner Auto Transport. Tool returned success.
✓Run #3linear_create_issue was called with title 'Resolve Road Runner Auto Transport delay — Marcus Webb vehicle shipping (BrightLoop Analytics)', clearly referencing all three key entities. The tool returned a result successfully.
✓Run #4linear_create_issue was called with title 'Marcus Webb (BrightLoop) vehicle shipping delay — Road Runner Auto Transport follow-up', clearly referencing Marcus Webb, BrightLoop, and Road Runner Auto Transport. The tool returned success.
✓Run #5linear_create_issue was called with title 'Resolve delayed vehicle shipping — Marcus Webb / BrightLoop (Road Runner Auto Transport)', explicitly referencing all three required entities. The tool returned success.
✓Run #6linear_create_issue was called with title 'BrightLoop vehicle shipping failure — Marcus Webb Honda Civic stranded at Indianapolis hub', explicitly referencing Marcus Webb, BrightLoop, and Road Runner Auto Transport in both the title and description. The tool returned success.
3 The Linear issue description (visible in linear_create_issue parameters) includes the Honda Civic stuck at Road Runner Auto Transport's Indianapolis transfer hub due to driver shortage (or similar key details).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The linear_create_issue description explicitly mentions 'Marcus Webb's 2019 Honda Civic', 'Road Runner's Indianapolis transfer hub', and 'driver no-show on the Boston leg' (driver shortage causing the delay).
✓Run #2The Linear issue description explicitly mentions: Honda Civic (2019 Honda Civic), the Indianapolis transfer hub, and driver shortage ('a driver called off on the final leg to Boston') as the root cause.
✓Run #3The linear_create_issue description explicitly mentions '2019 Honda Civic', 'Indianapolis transfer hub', and 'driver shortage on the final leg to Boston'.
✓Run #4The Linear issue description includes '2019 Honda Civic', 'Indianapolis transfer hub', and 'driver shortage on the Boston leg'. All required key details are present.
✓Run #5The Linear issue description contains 'Vehicle: 2019 Honda Civic', 'Indianapolis transfer hub', and 'driver called off on the Boston leg' (driver shortage). All three key details are present.
✓Run #6The Linear issue description includes the Honda Civic (2019 Honda Civic LX), the Indianapolis hub location (vehicle 'stranded at Indianapolis hub'), and the delay details. The criteria accepts 'driver shortage (or similar key details)' and the hub delay context is present.
4 The Linear issue description (visible in linear_create_issue parameters) includes the $1,450 bill outstanding ($1,100 base transport + $350 extended transit surcharge) or similar financial details.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The linear_create_issue description includes 'Original vehicle transport cost: $1,100', '$350' extended transit surcharge, and 'Total Road Runner bill: $1,450 (QuickBooks bill RR-2026-0471)' — both components and total are present.
✓Run #2The Linear issue description explicitly states: 'Road Runner bill (RR-2026-0471) shows $1,100 base + $350 extended transit surcharge = $1,450 total', covering both the base amount and the surcharge breakdown.
✓Run #3The linear_create_issue description states 'Total Road Runner bill: $1,450 (QuickBooks bill RR-2026-0471)' with a breakdown of '$1,100 base vehicle transport' and '$350 extended transit surcharge'.
✓Run #4The Linear issue description explicitly states '$1,100 base vehicle shipping cost invoiced on INV-2026-0308, plus $350 extended transit surcharge billed by Road Runner (QuickBooks bill RR-2026-0471, total $1,450)', covering both figures.
✓Run #5The Linear issue description explicitly states 'QuickBooks bill: RR-2026-0471 — $1,450 total ($1,100 base + $350 extended transit surcharge)' and 'Financial exposure: $1,450 vendor cost'. Both $1,450 total and the $1,100+$350 breakdown are present.
✓Run #6The Linear issue description explicitly includes '$1,450 (base $1,100 + $350 surcharge), QuickBooks bill RR-2026-0471', satisfying both the total and the breakdown requirement.
5 The Linear issue description (visible in linear_create_issue parameters) includes the April 18-20 revised delivery window or April 20 delivery deadline (or similar delivery timeline details).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The linear_create_issue description states 'Revised ETA: April 18-20' in the timeline section, clearly referencing the revised delivery window.
✓Run #2The Linear issue description mentions 'Road Runner offered a revised window of April 18–20' and 'stated the vehicle needed to arrive by April 20 for Marcus's April 22 team onboarding', covering both the revised delivery window and the April 20 deadline.
✓Run #3The linear_create_issue description includes 'Revised delivery window: April 18–20 (per Road Runner delay notification April 11)' and 'Client deadline: Vehicle needed by April 20'.
✓Run #4The Linear issue description includes 'Revised ETA: April 18–20 (tentative, unconfirmed)', meeting the requirement for the April 18-20 delivery window.
✓Run #5The Linear issue description includes 'Revised ETA (unconfirmed): April 18–20' and mentions Tessa Moreno expecting delivery by April 20 for employee onboarding April 22. The April 18-20 delivery window is clearly present.
✓Run #6The Linear issue description includes 'revised window: April 18–20 (unconfirmed)' and 'hard deadline missed: vehicle needed by April 20 for April 22 onboarding', satisfying the April 18-20 delivery timeline requirement.
6 A message was posted (via conversations_add_message) in the #operations Slack channel.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1conversations_add_message was called with channel_id '#operations'. The tool result file (tool_call_result_29.json) was returned, indicating success. The message content is extensive and clearly for the operations channel.
✓Run #2conversations_add_message was called with channel_id '#operations'. The trajectory confirms this was the operations channel. Tool returned success.

7 The #operations Slack message (visible in conversations_add_message payload) mentions the Heartland dispute resolution status (e.g., corrected invoice of $9,200, or $1,200 Marchetti cancellation fee retained, or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The #operations message payload explicitly mentions 'Heartland's proposed corrected invoice: $9,200', the $1,200 Marchetti cancellation fee retained, and the partial credits for Osei and Venkatesh.
✓Run #2The #operations Slack message explicitly references the Heartland dispute, $9,200 corrected total, Marchetti $1,200 cancellation fee retained (Section 4.2 MSA), and credits for Osei and Venkatesh.
✓Run #3The Slack message payload clearly references 'Proposed corrected invoice: $9,200', '$1,200 cancellation fee' for Marchetti, and credits for Osei and Venkatesh as part of the Heartland dispute status.
✓Run #4The #operations Slack message explicitly mentions 'Proposed corrected invoice: $9,200', the Marchetti $1,200 cancellation fee, and the credits for Osei and Venkatesh. The Heartland dispute status is covered in detail.
✓Run #5The Slack message contains detailed Heartland dispute information including $9,200 corrected invoice, $1,200 Marchetti cancellation fee retained, and the $3,600 in credits received. All key dispute status details are present.

8 The #operations Slack message mentions Heartland's rate increase (20% increase effective July 1, 2026, or similar).
outcome

The calendar searches returned empty results, so the agent created a new event rather than verifying an existing one. The criteria references an event 'scheduled for April 24, 2026', and the agent created a new one on April 28 instead. While the 4 coordinators are present in the new event, the pre-existing event may not have been updated.

tool selection

✓Run #6The agent used quickbooks_search_bills (multiple calls) and quickbooks_search_invoices to look up vendor bills and customer invoices, successfully finding the Road Runner bill RR-2026-0471 with the $1,450 total.

34 The model must search QuickBooks for the Road Runner Auto Transport vendor bill by passing criteria related to 'Road Runner' or 'auto transport' or 'vehicle' or the bill number 'RR-2026-0471' (or similar) when calling a QuickBooks bill search tool (e.g., quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_get_bill), rather than only searching for the BrightLoop customer invoice.
query construction
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called quickbooks_search_bills with {fetchAll: true}, which retrieves all vendor bills including the Road Runner bill (RR-2026-0471). The agent did not limit the QuickBooks search to BrightLoop only — the fetchAll approach surfaced all bills and the $1,450 Road Runner vendor bill was correctly identified. This satisfies the spirit of the criterion as the agent found the vendor bill rather than only looking at customer invoices.
✓Run #2The second quickbooks_search_bills call used fetchAll:true which returned all bills including RR-2026-0471 (Road Runner Auto Transport). The agent successfully found the Road Runner vendor bill with the $1,450 total, demonstrating it searched for the vendor bill, not just the BrightLoop customer invoice. The $350 surcharge was captured from this bill.
✓Run #3The agent called quickbooks_search_bills with fetchAll:true (not just BrightLoop), which would retrieve all vendor bills including RR-2026-0471 for Road Runner. The agent did not limit its QuickBooks search to BrightLoop only.
✓Run #4quickbooks_search_bills was called with fetchAll:true, which fetches all bills and would return the Road Runner vendor bill RR-2026-0471 without limiting to just BrightLoop. The agent did not restrict the search to 'BrightLoop' only.
✓Run #5quickbooks_search_bills was called with {"fetchAll": true}, which retrieves all bills and would surface the Road Runner vendor bill (RR-2026-0471). The agent then correctly identified the $1,450 amount including the $350 surcharge from this bill. This is not a BrightLoop-only search.
✓Run #6The agent called quickbooks_search_bills with fetchAll: true which returned all bills including the Road Runner vendor bill RR-2026-0471. While the search wasn't specifically filtered to 'Road Runner', the agent retrieved the correct vendor bill and used the $1,450 figure correctly, satisfying the intent of this criterion.

tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
